title: Location column
excerpt: Hi, i have just imported in a 30k database, and it dos not allow me to select a location column for the postcode, is there a way of importing in a location, as i believe this is not possible at the moment, will i have to…

posts.author: Joner007 (lynn jones)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, i have just imported in a 30k database, and it dos not allow me to select a location column for the postcode, is there a way of importing in a location, as i believe this is not possible at the moment, will i have to change them manually?

posts.author: MCautreels (Maarten Cautreels - Tryve)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Lynn,

The default monday import does indeed not support importing into a Location column. That’s why we’ve built the Location Importer which is an app in the marketplace that allows you to convert addresses in a Text column into a Location column.

Feel free to get in touch and schedule a short call with me so I can show it to you!

Best,

Maarten

posts.author: Joner007 (lynn jones)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Maarten

Is this free or is there a cost to this app?

Lynn

posts.author: Fario_Consulting (Fario Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Lynn,
Another workaround would be to use the Google Maps API to convert addresses into GPS coordinates. These coordinates can then be imported into your location column via the Monday API
Cheers,
Harsh

We develop custom solutions on Monday to help elevate your business. Reach out on farioconsulting.com
